title: Smoky Roasted Peruvian Beef Stir-fry
description: Tender roasted beef served in a smoky tamarind sauce and wrapped in fragrant pandan leaves, a romantic lomo saltado that is both Buddhist temple friendly and full of flavor.

ingredients.name: beef sirloin
ingredients.notes: sliced into 1cm thick strips for lomo saltado

instructions: Preheat oven to 200C and place 400g beef slices on a rack to roast.
Roast beef for 8 minutes until browned, then remove and set aside to rest.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan, add sliced onion, bell pepper and carrot, and stir for 3 minutes until tender.
Add diced tomato, cumin, smoked paprika, and stir for another 2 minutes.
Pour in tamarind paste, soy sauce, lime juice, salt, and black pepper; cook for 3 minutes until sauce reduces.
Slice rested beef into thin strips and add to the pan, sauté for 2 minutes to combine flavors.
Lay pandan leaves on a flat surface, spoon the beef mixture onto each leaf, roll tightly, and secure with kitchen twine.
Steam the wrapped parcels for 5 minutes, then serve hot beside a fresh cilantro garnish.
